message:
docs: complete ft branding sweep in remaining files

Replaces leftover "wa" references with "ft" across six files that
were missed in the earlier branding pass (fec6433, a48d054):

- crates/frankenterm/src/mcp.rs: module-level doc comment
- docs/config-profiles-spec.md: ~/.config/wa/ → ~/.config/ft/
- docs/ruleset-profiles-spec.md: ~/.config/wa/ → ~/.config/ft/
- fixtures/e2e/dummy_alt_screen.sh: policy comment
- fixtures/e2e/dummy_ignored_pane.sh: artifact + capture comments
- fuzz/src/lib.rs: crate-level doc comment

No behavioral changes — text-only fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -507,7 +507,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-2l27x.2","title":"Implement mux topology serializer","description":"Serialize WezTerm mux session topology (tabs, splits, pane positions) into a versioned JSON format for storage and reconstruction.

## Data model

### TopologySnapshot (Rust struct, serde)
```rust
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct TopologySnapshot {
    schema_version: u32,  // 1 initially, for forward compat
    captured_at: u64,     // epoch ms
    workspace_id: String, // from wezterm cli list --format json
    windows: Vec<WindowSnapshot>,
}

struct WindowSnapshot {
    window_id: u64,
    title: String,
    position: Option<(i32, i32)>,  // x, y
    size: (u32, u32),              // width, height in pixels
    tabs: Vec<TabSnapshot>,
    active_tab_index: usize,
}

struct TabSnapshot {
    tab_id: u64,
    title: String,
    pane_tree: PaneNode,  // recursive split tree
    active_pane_id: u64,
}

enum PaneNode {
    Leaf { pane_id: u64, rows: u16, cols: u16 },
    HSplit { children: Vec<(f64, PaneNode)> },  // (proportion, child)
    VSplit { children: Vec<(f64, PaneNode)> },
}
```

## Data collection
- Primary source: `wezterm cli list --format json` (gives pane_id, tab_id, window_id, size, cwd, title)
- Split tree reconstruction: infer from pane positions/sizes within each tab
  - Sort panes by position, detect split boundaries from aligned edges
  - Fallback: flat list if inference fails (degrade to "all panes in one tab, no split structure")
- Store the raw `wezterm cli list` JSON alongside the inferred topology for debugging

## Pane ID stability strategy
WezTerm assigns monotonically increasing pane IDs. After a crash+restart, all IDs change. The topology serializer must store enough info to reconstruct a mapping:
- Store (pane_id, cwd, title, command, terminal_size) tuples
- On restore, match old panes to new panes via (cwd, title, position_in_tree)
- If ambiguous, use terminal size as tiebreaker
- Keep a pane_id_mapping table or in-memory map during restore

## Deserialization & reconstruction
- Parse TopologySnapshot → reconstruction plan
- Reconstruction order: create tabs first (wezterm cli spawn), then splits (wezterm cli split-pane)
- Track old_pane_id → new_pane_id mapping throughout
- Handle partial restore: if a split-pane command fails, log warning and continue with remaining panes
- Emit a ReconstructionReport with (restored, failed, skipped) counts

## Unit tests required
- test_topology_roundtrip: serialize → deserialize for various layouts (single pane, 2x2 grid, deep nested splits, 10+ panes)
- test_split_tree_inference: given pane positions, correctly infer HSplit/VSplit tree
- test_split_tree_inference_ambiguous: degrade gracefully when positions don't form clean splits
- test_pane_matching_by_cwd: old panes matched to new panes via cwd
- test_pane_matching_ambiguous: two panes with same cwd, different sizes → size tiebreak
- test_empty_workspace: zero panes → valid empty TopologySnapshot
- test_schema_version_forward_compat: unknown fields ignored by older code

## Proptest
- Arbitrary PaneNode trees → roundtrip through serde
- Arbitrary window/tab layouts → reconstruction plan is valid

## Logging
- INFO: "Captured topology: {window_count} windows, {tab_count} tabs, {pane_count} panes"
- DEBUG: "Split tree inferred for tab {tab_id}: {tree_summary}"
- WARN: "Could not infer split structure for tab {tab_id}, falling back to flat layout"
- DEBUG: "Topology serialized: {bytes} bytes, hash={hash}"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealGate","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:22.578744Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:43:49.347888-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:43:49.347888-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["mux","serialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.2","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:22.578744Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2l27x.3","title":"Implement terminal state snapshot serializer","description":"Capture and serialize per-pane terminal state for crash-resilient restoration.

## Data model

### PaneStateSnapshot (Rust struct, serde)
```rust
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct PaneStateSnapshot {
    schema_version: u32,  // 1 initially
    pane_id: u64,
    captured_at: u64,     // epoch ms

    // Process info (best-effort)
    cwd: Option<String>,
    foreground_process: Option<ProcessInfo>,
    shell: Option<String>,  // e.g. "bash", "zsh", "fish"

    // Terminal state
    terminal: TerminalState,

    // Scrollback linkage
    scrollback_ref: Option<ScrollbackRef>,

    // Agent context (populated by .10 if agent detected)
    agent: Option<AgentMetadata>,
}

struct ProcessInfo {
    name: String,         // e.g. "claude-code"
    pid: Option<u32>,
    argv: Option<Vec<String>>,  // first 5 args max
}

struct TerminalState {
    rows: u16,
    cols: u16,
    cursor_row: u16,
    cursor_col: u16,
    is_alt_screen: bool,
    title: String,
}

struct ScrollbackRef {
    output_segments_seq: i64,  // last captured sequence number
    total_lines_captured: u64,
    last_capture_at: u64,      // epoch ms
}
```

## Data collection sources
- **cwd**: `wezterm cli list` cwd field (reliable)
- **foreground process**: Linux: /proc/<pid>/stat via pane's leader PID; macOS: sysctl kern.proc.pid
  - WezTerm exposes pane.foreground_process_name in Lua, but CLI doesn't — fall back to procfs
  - If unavailable, set to None (non-fatal)
- **terminal state**: from existing ScreenStateTracker (alt-screen) + wezterm cli list (title, size)
  - Cursor position: parse from wezterm cli get-text output if DSR escape available, else estimate from last output position
- **scrollback ref**: query output_segments table for max(seq) WHERE pane_id = ?

## Environment variable capture
Capture a curated set of env vars (not the full environment — privacy):
- PATH, HOME, SHELL, TERM, LANG, EDITOR, FT_WORKSPACE, FT_OUTPUT_FORMAT
- Redact any var matching SECRET|TOKEN|KEY|PASSWORD|CREDENTIAL patterns via existing Redactor
- Store as JSON object in env_json column

## Serialization format
- JSON with deny_unknown_fields for strict deserialization
- schema_version field allows future additions without breaking old checkpoints
- Total size budget: 64KB per pane (log warning if exceeded, truncate env/argv)

## Unit tests required
- test_pane_state_roundtrip: serialize → deserialize for various states
- test_alt_screen_detection: ScreenStateTracker integration
- test_scrollback_ref_linkage: verify seq number matches output_segments
- test_process_info_collection: mock /proc filesystem (Linux) / sysctl (macOS)
- test_env_redaction: SECRET vars are redacted, safe vars preserved
- test_size_budget: warn and truncate when pane state exceeds 64KB
- test_schema_version_forward_compat: v2 data read by v1 code ignores unknown fields

## Logging
- DEBUG: "Captured state for pane {pane_id}: cwd={cwd}, process={process}, alt_screen={alt}"
- DEBUG: "Scrollback ref for pane {pane_id}: seq={seq}, lines={lines}"
- WARN: "Could not determine foreground process for pane {pane_id}: {reason}"
- WARN: "Pane {pane_id} state exceeds 64KB budget ({actual_bytes}), truncating"
- TRACE: "Environment capture for pane {pane_id}: {var_count} vars, {redacted_count} redacted"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:31.758245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:43:49.400299-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:43:49.400299-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["serialization","terminal-state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.3","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:31.758245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2l27x.4","title":"Implement periodic checkpoint engine","description":"Add an automatic checkpoint loop to the frankenterm watcher that periodically serializes session state.

NOTE: Graceful shutdown checkpoint is handled by .9 (separate concern with signal handler complexity).

## Design
- Run as a tokio::spawn task alongside the existing capture loop in runtime.rs
- Owned by the ObservationRuntime — starts when watcher starts, stops when watcher stops
- Communicates via a dedicated mpsc channel for checkpoint requests

## Checkpoint loop
```
loop {
    tokio::select! {
        _ = interval.tick() => { do_periodic_checkpoint().await; }
        Some(event) = event_rx.recv() => { do_event_checkpoint(event).await; }
        _ = shutdown_rx.recv() => { break; }  // clean shutdown handled by .9
    }
}
```

## Checkpoint types
- **periodic**: Fixed interval (default 1000ms, configurable via ft.toml [session.checkpoint_interval_ms])
  - 500ms was too aggressive — WAL fsync at FULL mode costs ~2ms, so 500ms interval = 0.4% overhead
  - 1000ms is a good default — at most 1 second of session state loss
- **event**: Triggered by topology changes detected by the capture loop:
  - Pane created/destroyed (pane count changed between captures)
  - Tab created/destroyed
  - Window created/destroyed
  - Split layout changed (pane sizes changed significantly)
- NOT shutdown — that's .9's responsibility

## Deduplication
- Compute BLAKE3 hash of (topology_json || all pane_state_json concatenated)
- Compare with last checkpoint's state_hash
- If identical, skip write, update last_checkpoint_at on mux_sessions only
- Log at DEBUG level: "Checkpoint skipped (no state change), hash={hash}"

## Retention
- Keep last N checkpoints per session (default: 10, configurable via [session.checkpoint_retention])
- Prune older checkpoints in the same transaction as the new write
- Cascade delete mux_pane_state rows via FK ON DELETE CASCADE
- Log at DEBUG: "Pruned {count} old checkpoints, retained {retained}"

## Config additions to ft.toml
```toml
[session]
# Master switch
checkpoint_enabled = true
# Interval between periodic checkpoints (ms)
checkpoint_interval_ms = 1000
# Max checkpoints retained per session
checkpoint_retention = 10
# Durability mode for session writes
synchronous_mode = "full"  # "full" = fsync, "normal" = faster but risk window
```

## Two-connection architecture
- Connection 1 (session_conn): PRAGMA synchronous = FULL, used only for session table writes
- Connection 2 (output_conn): PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL, used for output_segments (existing)
- Both share the same database file, both in WAL mode
- This avoids penalizing output capture throughput with session durability requirements

## Unit tests required
- test_periodic_checkpoint: verify checkpoints written at expected intervals
- test_event_checkpoint_pane_created: pane count change triggers immediate checkpoint
- test_dedup_skips_identical: two identical states → only one checkpoint row
- test_dedup_writes_on_change: state change → new checkpoint row
- test_retention_prunes_old: after N+1 checkpoints, oldest is deleted
- test_retention_cascades_pane_state: pruned checkpoint's pane_state rows also deleted
- test_two_connections_isolation: FULL sync on session_conn doesn't affect output_conn perf
- test_config_defaults: missing [session] section → sensible defaults
- test_config_disabled: checkpoint_enabled=false → no checkpoint loop started

## Logging
- INFO on startup: "Session checkpoint engine started (interval={interval_ms}ms, retention={retention})"
- INFO: "Checkpoint #{id} written: {pane_count} panes, {bytes} bytes, type={type}"
- DEBUG: "Checkpoint skipped (no state change since #{last_id})"
- DEBUG: "Pruned {count} old checkpoints (retention={retention})"
- WARN: "Checkpoint write took {elapsed_ms}ms (threshold: 100ms)" — if slow
- ERROR: "Checkpoint failed: {error}" — with retry logic (max 3 retries, exponential backoff)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:42.740558Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:05:02.712984-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:05:02.712984-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in snapshot_engine.rs: SnapshotEngine::run_periodic() runs as tokio task, captures topology + pane state on configurable interval (default 300s), skips unchanged state via hash dedup, persists to SQLite with retention cleanup. 12 tests pass.","labels":["checkpoint","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:42.740558Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:29.95721Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.052473Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.146621Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-2l27x.5","title":"Implement session restore on startup","description":"On ft watch startup, detect prior session state and perform automatic restoration.

## Startup detection flow
1. Open database, check mux_sessions for sessions where shutdown_clean = 0
2. If multiple unclean sessions exist, pick the most recent (by last_checkpoint_at)
3. Load the latest session_checkpoint for that session
4. Load all mux_pane_state rows for that checkpoint
5. Display restore summary (or auto-restore if configured)

## Restore sequence (detailed)

### Step 1: Verify WezTerm is running
- wezterm cli list must succeed
- If WezTerm has existing panes, warn user: "WezTerm has {N} existing panes. Restore will create new panes alongside them."

### Step 2: Recreate tab/split topology
- Parse TopologySnapshot from topology_json
- For each window:
  - For each tab: wezterm cli spawn --new-tab (capture new tab_id)
  - For each split in tab: wezterm cli split-pane --right/--bottom with appropriate percentages
  - Record old_pane_id → new_pane_id mapping at each step

### Step 3: Restore working directories
- For each pane: send `cd {cwd}\n` via wezterm cli send-text
- Wait for shell prompt (use existing wait-for pattern: shell prompt detection)
- If cwd doesn't exist, log warning and skip

### Step 4: Scrollback replay strategy
This is the hardest part. Options evaluated:

**Option A: Output injection via wezterm cli send-text** — BAD
- send-text goes to the process stdin, not the terminal display
- Would execute commands, not display output
- REJECTED

**Option B: Direct mux protocol write** — BEST for native-wezterm feature
- Use the vendored DirectMuxClient to write directly to pane output
- WriteToPane PDU can inject text that appears as terminal output
- Preserves escape sequences (colors, cursor movement)
- Only works with native-wezterm feature enabled

**Option C: Write to pane via /dev/pts** — FRAGILE
- Write to the slave PTY device
- Requires knowing the PTY path (platform-specific)
- REJECTED (too fragile)

**Option D: Skip scrollback, show summary** — FALLBACK
- Don't replay scrollback; instead, show a banner in each pane:
  "═══ Session restored from checkpoint {id} at {time} ═══"
  "═══ Previous output available via: ft session show {id} --pane {old_pane_id} ═══"
- This is the safe default; scrollback replay is opt-in via restore_scrollback config

**Decision**: Default to Option D (banner). If native-wezterm feature is enabled AND restore_scrollback = true, use Option B. Never use A or C.

### Step 5: Restore agent context
- If pane had agent_metadata_json (from .10), display it in the restore banner:
  "═══ Previously running: Claude Code (session abc123, state: idle) ═══"
- Do NOT auto-restart agents (too dangerous)

### Step 6: Update pane ID mappings
- Write a pane_id_mapping to session_checkpoints metadata:
  { "old_to_new": { "0": "5", "1": "6", "2": "7" } }
- Update the ft watcher's in-memory pane tracking to use new IDs
- The next checkpoint will use the new pane IDs

### Step 7: Mark session as restored
- Insert a new checkpoint with type='startup' and the new topology
- Mark the old session's shutdown_clean = 1 (retroactively, since we recovered)

## Config
```toml
[session]
# Restore behavior on startup
auto_restore = false       # true = skip prompt, always restore
restore_scrollback = false # true = replay scrollback via mux protocol (requires native-wezterm)
restore_max_lines = 5000   # max scrollback lines to replay per pane
```

## Error handling
- Partial restore: log which panes couldn't be recreated, continue with the rest
- Corrupt checkpoint (BLAKE3 mismatch): fall back to previous checkpoint
- All checkpoints corrupt: start fresh, produce diagnostic bundle
- WezTerm command failures: retry once, then skip that pane

## Integration tests required
- test_detect_unclean_session: create session with shutdown_clean=0, verify detection
- test_restore_single_pane: checkpoint → restore → verify pane exists with correct cwd
- test_restore_split_layout: 2x2 grid → restore → verify 4 panes with correct sizes
- test_restore_partial_failure: one split-pane fails → other panes still restored
- test_restore_banner_displayed: verify banner text appears in restored panes
- test_restore_pane_id_mapping: old IDs correctly mapped to new IDs
- test_corrupt_checkpoint_fallback: corrupt latest → falls back to previous
- test_no_unclean_sessions: clean database → no restore prompt
- test_auto_restore_config: auto_restore=true → no prompt, immediate restore

## Logging
- INFO: "Detected unclean session {session_id} (last checkpoint: {time})"
- INFO: "Restoring session: {pane_count} panes across {tab_count} tabs"
- INFO: "Pane {old_id} → {new_id}: cwd={cwd}, agent={agent_type}"
- INFO: "Session restore complete: {restored}/{total} panes restored in {elapsed_ms}ms"
- WARN: "Failed to restore pane {old_id}: {error}"
- WARN: "Checkpoint {id} failed integrity check (expected={expected}, got={got}), trying previous"
- ERROR: "Session restore failed completely: {error}" → produce crash bundle
- DEBUG: "Scrollback replay: {lines} lines injected into pane {new_id} via mux protocol"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:53.314385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.646152Z","labels":["restore","startup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.5","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:53.314385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.5","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.236403Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.5","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.646112Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

- Insert a new checkpoint with type='startup' and the new topology

restore_max_lines = 5000   # max scrollback lines to replay per pane

 {"id":"wa-2l27x.6","title":"Add ft session CLI subcommands","description":"Add CLI subcommands for manual session management.

## Commands

### Read-only (only need tables .1)
ft session list              # List all saved sessions with checkpoint counts
ft session show <session_id> # Show session topology, pane states, checkpoint history
ft session show <session_id> --pane <pane_id>  # Show specific pane's last state + scrollback ref
ft session diff <id1> <id2>  # Compare two checkpoints (topology/pane state changes)

### Write (need restore logic .5)
ft session restore [session_id] [--checkpoint <id>]  # Restore a specific session/checkpoint
ft session save              # Force immediate checkpoint (triggers .4's event channel)
ft session delete <session_id> [--force]  # Delete a session and all its checkpoints
ft session export <session_id> <path>  # Export session as self-contained JSON bundle
ft session import <path>     # Import a session bundle (for cross-machine migration)

### Diagnostic
ft session doctor            # Check session health (corrupt checkpoints, orphaned pane states)

## Robot mode
All commands support --format json/toon for agent consumption.
Response envelope: { ok, data, error, elapsed_ms, version }

## Output format for ft session list
```
Session ID      | Created     | Last Checkpoint | Panes | Status
ft-abc123       | 2026-02-10  | 2m ago          | 12    | active
ft-def456       | 2026-02-09  | 1d ago          | 8     | unclean (crash)
ft-ghi789       | 2026-02-08  | 2d ago          | 5     | clean (shutdown)
```

## Unit tests required
- test_session_list_empty: no sessions → empty table
- test_session_list_with_data: multiple sessions → correct counts and statuses
- test_session_show_topology: verify topology tree rendered correctly
- test_session_show_pane: specific pane state displayed with scrollback ref
- test_session_diff: two checkpoints with different pane counts → diff shown
- test_session_export_import_roundtrip: export → import → identical data
- test_session_doctor_corrupt: corrupt checkpoint detected and reported
- test_robot_mode_json: all commands produce valid JSON envelope

## Logging
- DEBUG: "Session list query returned {count} sessions"
- INFO: "Session {id} exported to {path} ({bytes} bytes)"
- WARN: "Session doctor found {count} corrupt checkpoints"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:00.637348Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.729315Z","labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.6","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:00.637348Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.6","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:45:02.726754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.6","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.729278Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2l27x.7","title":"E2E test suite & scripts with detailed logging","description":"End-to-end test scripts that verify the full session persistence lifecycle, with detailed structured logging for diagnosis.

## E2E test script: scripts/e2e_session_persistence.sh

### Test harness setup
- Creates isolated FT_WORKSPACE in /tmp/ft-e2e-session-XXXXXX
- Starts a real WezTerm mux server (or uses existing)
- Starts ft watch in foreground mode with --log-level=debug --log-format=json
- All output captured to $WORKSPACE/logs/

### Test scenarios

#### Scenario 1: Basic checkpoint-crash-restore cycle
```
1. Start ft watch
2. Create 3 panes via wezterm cli split-pane
3. cd to different directories in each pane
4. Wait for at least 2 periodic checkpoints
5. Verify: ft session list shows 1 active session with 3 panes
6. Kill ft watch with SIGKILL (simulates crash — no graceful shutdown)
7. Verify: database has shutdown_clean = 0
8. Start ft watch again
9. Verify: restore prompt appears (or auto-restores if configured)
10. Verify: 3 new panes created with correct cwds
11. Verify: pane ID mapping recorded in checkpoint metadata
```
Exit: PASS/FAIL with detailed log output on failure

#### Scenario 2: Complex topology preservation
```
1. Create a 2x3 grid (2 rows, 3 columns via nested splits)
2. Set unique titles and cwds for each pane
3. Checkpoint
4. Kill + restart
5. Verify: all 6 panes restored with correct positions
6. Verify: split proportions within 10% of original
```

#### Scenario 3: Partial restore resilience
```
1. Create 4 panes
2. Checkpoint
3. Kill ft watch
4. Delete one of the cwds (rm -rf one test directory)
5. Restart ft watch with restore
6. Verify: 3 panes restored, 1 skipped with warning
7. Verify: restore completes (doesn't abort on partial failure)
```

#### Scenario 4: Checkpoint deduplication efficiency
```
1. Create 2 panes, wait for steady state
2. Record checkpoint count over 10 seconds
3. Verify: only 1-2 checkpoints written (dedup working)
4. Send text to a pane (changes state)
5. Verify: new checkpoint written immediately (event trigger)
```

#### Scenario 5: Graceful shutdown vs crash
```
1. Start ft watch, create panes
2. ft stop (graceful)
3. Verify: shutdown_clean = 1 in database
4. Restart
5. Verify: NO restore prompt (clean shutdown)
```

#### Scenario 6: Multiple crash recovery
```
1. Create session, checkpoint, SIGKILL
2. Restart, restore
3. Add more panes, checkpoint, SIGKILL again
4. Restart, restore
5. Verify: latest topology (with added panes) is restored
6. Verify: ft session list shows correct history
```

#### Scenario 7: Checkpoint retention
```
1. Set checkpoint_retention = 3
2. Trigger 10 checkpoints (with state changes)
3. Verify: only 3 checkpoints remain in database
4. Verify: oldest checkpoints and their pane_state rows are deleted
```

### Logging verification
Each scenario verifies that expected log lines appear in the JSON log output:
- Checkpoint written log with pane count and byte size
- Checkpoint skipped log (dedup)
- Restore detected log with session ID
- Pane restore log with old_id → new_id mapping
- Error/warning logs for partial failures

### Script output format
```
═══════════════════════════════════════
ft Session Persistence E2E Test Suite
═══════════════════════════════════════

[1/7] Basic checkpoint-crash-restore ............ PASS (3.2s)
[2/7] Complex topology preservation ............ PASS (4.1s)
[3/7] Partial restore resilience ............... PASS (2.8s)
[4/7] Checkpoint deduplication ................. PASS (12.1s)
[5/7] Graceful shutdown vs crash ............... PASS (2.5s)
[6/7] Multiple crash recovery .................. PASS (5.3s)
[7/7] Checkpoint retention .................... PASS (8.2s)

═══════════════════════════════════════
Results: 7/7 passed, 0 failed
Logs: /tmp/ft-e2e-session-abc123/logs/
═══════════════════════════════════════
```

### Failure output
On failure, automatically dumps:
- Last 50 lines of ft watch JSON log
- ft session list output
- ft session doctor output
- Database schema version and table row counts

## Integration with CI
- Add to .github/workflows/ci.yml as a separate job
- Requires WezTerm installed (feature-gated, skip if not available)
- Timeout: 5 minutes

## Dependencies
- Depends on .5 (restore) and .9 (graceful shutdown) since scenarios test both","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:10.409219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.561674Z","labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.7","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:10.409219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.7","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.493454Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.7","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.476849Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.7","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.56164Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2l27x.8","title":"FrankenSQLite migration plan","description":"Plan and prepare for eventual migration from rusqlite to frankensqlite as the storage backend.

## Precondition
frankensqlite (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite) must complete Phase 5+ (WAL/B-tree/MVCC wired as default execution backend, not just tested components).

## Migration benefits
- MVCC concurrent writers: 50+ agent panes writing simultaneously without single-writer bottleneck
- RaptorQ self-healing WAL: survives torn writes and bit-flips after power loss
- Native mode time-travel: replay any terminal session at any point in time
- XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption at rest (session state is sensitive)

## Migration tasks (when ready)
1. Add frankensqlite as workspace dependency (it's a 23-crate workspace — evaluate binary size impact)
2. Create storage backend trait to abstract rusqlite vs frankensqlite
3. Implement frankensqlite backend behind feature flag
4. Benchmark: single-writer throughput, concurrent writer throughput, checkpoint latency
5. Migration tool: convert existing rusqlite .db to frankensqlite format
6. Gradual rollout: feature flag default off, test in staging

## Status: DEFERRED until frankensqlite Phase 5+ ships","status":"deferred","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:22.326998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:35.12062Z","labels":["deferred","frankensqlite","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.8","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:22.326998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.8","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.585744Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-ogc.7","title":"Track 5: Advanced - Custom patterns and multi-agent coordination (20 min)","description":"# Track 5: Advanced

## Purpose
Teach power users to extend wa with custom patterns, manage multi-agent setups, and use advanced features.

## Target Audience
Experienced users who want to customize wa for their specific workflows.

## Exercise Sequence

### Exercise 5.1: Custom Pattern Basics
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Pattern pack structure, rule format, matching semantics
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Exercise 5.2: Create a Pattern Rule
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: User creates a simple pattern rule
- **Content**: Write rule → test → save to custom pack
- **Duration**: 180 seconds

### Exercise 5.3: Test Custom Pattern
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: `wa rules test "your custom trigger text"`
- **Success**: Custom rule matches
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Exercise 5.4: Multi-Pane Overview
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Managing multiple agents across panes
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 5.5: Pane Reservations
- **Type**: Interactive (simulated)
- **Action**: User reserves a pane for exclusive workflow
- **Content**: `wa robot reserve --pane 0 --ttl 60`
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Exercise 5.6: Event Correlation
- **Type**: Information + Demo
- **Content**: How events across panes relate
- **Action**: View timeline showing cross-pane activity
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Exercise 5.7: FTS Power User
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: Complex FTS queries with boolean ops
- **Content**: `wa query "error AND codex NOT timeout"`
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Exercise 5.8: Export and Analysis
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: `wa export --format jsonl`
- **Content**: Offline analysis patterns
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Exercise 5.9: wa why (Explainability)
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: `wa why "PolicyDecision::Denied"`
- **Content**: Understanding wa's decisions
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Track Completion
- Achievement: "wa Master"
- Summary of all skills learned

## Testing
- Custom pattern creation must work in sandbox
- All FTS examples must return results
- Export must produce valid JSONL

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 9 exercises implemented
- [ ] Custom pattern workflow is complete
- [ ] Multi-pane concepts are clearly explained
- [ ] FTS advanced queries are demonstrated
- [ ] Export produces valid output
- [ ] Track completion shows full achievement list","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:47:07.65615415Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:18:13.982588895Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:18:13.982522361Z","close_reason":"done"}
 {"id":"wa-ogc.8","title":"Sandbox mode: simulated WezTerm and safe execution for tutorial","description":"# Sandbox mode for tutorial

## Purpose
Provide a safe, simulated environment where tutorial exercises run without affecting real panes or executing dangerous commands.

## Relationship to Simulation Mode (wa-a0c)
**IMPORTANT**: This task builds on the simulation infrastructure from wa-a0c.

The tutorial sandbox is a **pre-configured simulation scenario** that:
1. Uses `MockWezterm` from wa-a0c for pane simulation
2. Uses the scenario system from wa-a0c.3 for mock data
3. Adds tutorial-specific features (visual indicators, command hints)

This is NOT a separate mock implementation - it reuses and extends wa-a0c.

## Background
Users may:
- Not have WezTerm running
- Not have agents in their panes
- Be afraid to let tutorial "do things"

Sandbox mode addresses all these concerns by using simulation.

## Implementation Details

### Sandbox as Simulation Wrapper
```rust
pub struct TutorialSandbox {
    /// Reuses simulation infrastructure from wa-a0c
    simulation: MockWezterm,
    
    /// Tutorial-specific state
    current_exercise: Option<ExerciseId>,
    hints_shown: Vec<HintId>,
    command_log: Vec<SandboxCommand>,
}

impl TutorialSandbox {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        // Load tutorial-specific scenario
        let scenario = Scenario::load_builtin("tutorial/sandbox.yaml").unwrap();
        let mut simulation = MockWezterm::new();
        scenario.apply_to(&simulation).unwrap();
        
        Self {
            simulation,
            current_exercise: None,
            hints_shown: vec\![],
            command_log: vec\![],
        }
    }
}
```

### Tutorial Sandbox Scenario
```yaml
# scenarios/tutorial/sandbox.yaml
name: tutorial_sandbox
description: "Pre-configured environment for wa learn exercises"

panes:
  - id: 0
    title: "Local Shell"
    domain: "local"
    cwd: "/home/user/projects"
    
  - id: 1
    title: "Codex Agent"
    domain: "local"
    agent_type: codex
    initial_content: |
      codex> Ready to help with your project.
      What would you like to work on?

  - id: 2
    title: "Claude Code"
    domain: "local"
    agent_type: claude_code
    initial_content: |
      claude> Analyzing your codebase...

# Inject events based on exercise triggers
exercise_events:
  track1_ex2:  # When user reaches this exercise
    - at: 0s
      pane: 1
      action: append
      content: |
        [Usage Warning]
        Approaching daily usage limit.
```

### Visual Indicators
- `[SANDBOX]` prefix on all output
- Distinct colors for simulated vs real
- Clear "exit sandbox" instructions
- Hint tooltips for tutorial context

### Command Logging
```rust
impl TutorialSandbox {
    pub fn execute(&mut self, cmd: &WaCommand) -> Result<String> {
        // Log for tutorial feedback
        self.command_log.push(SandboxCommand {
            command: cmd.clone(),
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
            exercise: self.current_exercise.clone(),
        });
        
        // Execute against mock
        let result = self.simulation.execute(cmd).await?;
        
        // Wrap with sandbox indicator
        Ok(format\!("[SANDBOX] {}", result))
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Unit tests verify sandbox uses simulation correctly
- Tests for tutorial-specific features (hints, logging)
- E2E tests for full sandbox flow in tutorial context

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sandbox uses MockWezterm from wa-a0c
- [ ] Tutorial scenario provides realistic mock data
- [ ] Visual indicators clearly show sandbox mode
- [ ] Command logging tracks tutorial progress
- [ ] Exercise-triggered events work
- [ ] Tests verify integration with simulation

 {"id":"wa-ogc.9","title":"Achievement system: track milestones and motivate tutorial completion","description":"# Achievement system

## Purpose
Gamify the tutorial experience with achievements that motivate users to explore and complete tracks.

## Background
Achievements provide:
- Clear milestones and progress indicators
- Motivation to explore features
- Satisfying feedback loop
- Shareable accomplishments

## Implementation Details

### Achievement Types
```rust
pub struct Achievement {
    id: AchievementId,
    name: String,
    description: String,
    icon: char,      // Emoji or ASCII art
    rarity: Rarity,  // Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic
    unlocked_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
}

pub enum AchievementId {
    // Track completions
    FirstWatch,       // Complete Track 1
    PatternDetective, // Complete Track 2
    WorkflowWizard,   // Complete Track 3
    RobotOperator,    // Complete Track 4
    WaMaster,         // Complete Track 5
    
    // Hidden achievements
    SpeedRunner,      // Complete Track 1 in <3 min
    NightOwl,         // Complete exercise after midnight
    ExplorerPro,      // Use 10 different commands
    HelpfulHint,      // Use `wa learn --help-me` 5 times
}
```

### Display
```
╭──────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ 🏆 Achievement Unlocked!                 │
│                                          │
│ ⭐ Pattern Detective                     │
│ "Understood event detection and patterns"│
│                                          │
│ [2/5 tracks completed]                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Persistence
Achievements stored in progress file:
```json
{
  "achievements": [
    {"id": "first_watch", "unlocked_at": "2026-01-15T10:05:00Z"},
    {"id": "pattern_detective", "unlocked_at": "2026-01-15T10:15:00Z"}
  ]
}
```

### Secret Achievements
Some achievements are hidden until unlocked:
- SpeedRunner
- NightOwl
- Encourages exploration

## Testing
- Unit tests for achievement unlock conditions
- Golden tests for achievement display
- Integration tests for persistence

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 10+ achievements defined
- [ ] Achievement unlock display is attractive
- [ ] Progress persisted across sessions
- [ ] Secret achievements hidden until unlocked
- [ ] `wa learn --achievements` shows collection
- [ ] Tests cover unlock conditions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:47:35.40273314Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:17:44.360222469Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T03:17:44.360088701Z"}
-{"id":"wa-okyhm","title":"[EPIC] Mine Zellij codebase for FrankenTerm mux server improvements","description":"# Mine Zellij for FrankenTerm Mux Server Improvements

## Why Zellij?
Zellij is a Rust-based terminal multiplexer — the closest open-source analog to FrankenTerm's mux server layer. While Ghostty (wa-3bja) gives us rendering/I/O insights, Zellij gives us **multiplexer architecture** insights: session management, pane orchestration, plugin extensibility, and multi-client coordination.

## Reference repo
Cloned to legacy_zellij/ (gitignored). Upstream: https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij

## Key areas to study
1. **Session architecture** — How Zellij manages sessions, reconnection, and persistence (directly relevant to wa-rsaf session persistence epic)
2. **Pane/tab management** — Layout engine, split topology, floating panes, stacked panes
3. **Plugin system (WASM)** — Zellij uses WASM plugins for extensibility — could replace or complement our Lua/MCP approach
4. **IPC and client-server protocol** — How CLI communicates with the server process (compare to our Unix socket mux protocol)
5. **Performance patterns** — How Zellij handles high-throughput output, scrollback, and resource management
6. **Multi-client support** — How multiple clients attach to the same session (relevant to agent swarm coordination)

## Relationship to other epics
- wa-3bja (Ghostty): rendering and I/O pipeline focus
- wa-3kxe (Fork Hardening): fixing WezTerm mux server root causes
- wa-rsaf (Session Persistence): snapshot/restore, which Zellij handles natively
- wa-e34d9 (asupersync): async runtime, which Zellij's architecture can inform

## Expected output
Concrete, actionable improvement ideas for FrankenTerm's mux server — not just a summary of how Zellij works, but specific "we should steal this because..." recommendations with implementation sketches.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:17.790328Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:17.790328Z"}

 {"id":"wa-p3i","title":"E2E script: watch-and-notify mode (--notify-only events → notifications)","description":"# E2E script: watch-and-notify mode (`wa watch --notify-only`)

## Goal
Validate end-to-end that notify-only mode:
- detects events and emits notifications
- **does not auto-handle** (no workflows run, events remain unhandled)
- respects notify filters and throttling
- includes suggested actions in notifications

This is the integration proof for notify-only mode (`wa-spi`) + notifications (`wa-psm`) under the standard E2E harness.

## Key constraints
- Deterministic: **no fixed `sleep N`**. Use bounded waits (polling with timeouts) and a quiescence detector.
- Safe shutdown: watcher processes must be stopped gracefully and reliably (SIGINT/`wa stop`), with bounded waits.
- No global config mutation: use an isolated E2E workspace/config.
- Artifacts must be redacted (no transcript leakage).

## Test setup
- Start a harness-owned mock webhook server with:
  - `/received` to read captured notifications as JSON
  - `/attempt_count` (optional)
  - request logging to artifacts
- Start WezTerm mux + dummy panes that can deterministically emit known events.
- Start `wa watch` in notify-only mode in a dedicated E2E workspace:
  - `wa watch --notify-only …`
  - notifications configured to point to the mock server

## Scenarios

### 1) Notify-only mode does not auto-handle
- Start watcher: `wa watch --notify-only --workspace <e2e> …`
- Trigger a known event that would normally be auto-handled (e.g., compaction):
  - via dummy pane output (preferred) OR a harness `wa e2e emit` helper
- Wait until mock server has ≥1 received payload (poll with timeout).
- Assertions:
  - notification payload exists and references the correct `rule_id` / pane
  - `wa events --unhandled --format json` still lists the event (still unhandled)
  - `wa workflow executions` (or DB evidence) shows **no** workflow ran for that event

### 2) Notification filter
- Start watcher with a filter:
  - `wa watch --notify-only --notify-filter "usage_limit" …`
- Trigger two events:
  - one matching filter (usage limit)
  - one non-matching (compaction)
- Wait for quiescence (bounded).
- Assert mock server received exactly one notification, for the matching event.

### 3) Throttling in notify-only mode
- Configure a low throttle budget in the E2E config.
- Trigger a burst of N events quickly (deterministic dummy output).
- Wait for quiescence (bounded).
- Assert:
  - delivered notifications ≤ configured budget
  - watcher logs contain explicit “throttled” markers for skipped notifications

### 4) Suggested action included
- Trigger compaction.
- Wait for first notification.
- Assert the notification body includes a suggested remediation (e.g., `wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane <id>`).

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`) with prerequisites and default inclusion status.

## Artifacts
- `wa_watch_notify_only.log`
- `notifications_received.json` (redacted)
- `events_unhandled.json`
- `workflow_executions_slice.json` (or other “no workflow ran” evidence)
- `mock_server.log`

## Logging contract
Every step logs:
- case/scenario name
- watcher PID/workspace
- mock server bind address
- timeouts used

Example:
```
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] watcher started workspace=e2e pid=12345 notify_only=true filter=usage_limit
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] emit rule_id=codex.usage_limit_reached pane=3
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] wait received>=1 timeout=10s
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] assert event still unhandled: OK
```

## Testing
- Determinism:
  - no fixed sleeps
  - all waits are “wait until condition” with explicit timeouts
- Correctness:
  - notification exists and matches expected `rule_id` + `pane_id`
  - event remains unhandled (no workflow execution)
  - filter and throttling behave as configured
- Shutdown reliability:
  - stop watcher via `wa stop --workspace <e2e>` when available, otherwise SIGINT + bounded wait
  - artifacts always include watcher exit status and last log lines

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Notify-only mode delivers notifications for detected events.
- [ ] Notify-only mode does not auto-handle (events remain unhandled, no workflows executed).
- [ ] Filters and throttling work deterministically.
- [ ] Notifications include suggested actions.
- [ ] Artifacts/logs are sufficient to debug failures.","notes":"Completed: added watch_notify_only scenario to E2E registry + harness (notify-only baseline, filter, throttling, suggested action checks) and updated e2e integration checklist. E2E script not executed here (requires WezTerm/mux).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderSnow","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:15.541602048Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:43:53.93705561Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:43:53.93688875Z"}
 {"id":"wa-p48pw","title":"Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync

## Background
mux_client.rs (997 LOC) is the most complex async module. Manages persistent Unix socket connections with custom binary protocol.

## Current tokio usage
- tokio::net::UnixStream for socket connection
- tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} for framed I/O
- tokio::time::timeout for 5s connect/read/write timeouts
- tokio::sync::mpsc for pane output subscription
- tokio::sync::watch for cancellation signaling
- tokio::spawn + tokio::select! for background polling

## Migration order
1. Replace UnixStream with asupersync net
2. Replace AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt with asupersync I/O traits
3. Replace timeouts with Budget::deadline(Duration::from_secs(5))
4. Replace mpsc/watch with asupersync channels (two-phase send)
5. Replace tokio::spawn with scope.spawn for pane output subscription
6. Replace select! with asupersync::combinator::select for poll+cancel

## Critical: Wire protocol MUST NOT change
PDU framing: 4-byte big-endian length prefix + PDU type byte + payload.
The framing logic must produce identical wire format. This is non-negotiable.

## subscribe_pane_output() migration (hardest part)
```rust
// Becomes a scoped task:
cx.region(|scope| async {
    let (tx, rx) = asupersync::channel::mpsc::channel(capacity);
    scope.spawn(|cx| async move {
        loop {
            cx.checkpoint()?;  // Cancellation point
            let delta = fetch_pane_delta(cx).await?;
            let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;
            permit.send(delta);
        }
    });
    rx
}).await
```

## Comprehensive unit tests (LabRuntime + real sockets)
1. **Connect**: connect to mock Unix socket, verify stream established
2. **PDU round-trip**: write PDU frame, read back, verify bit-for-bit identical
3. **PDU partial read**: simulate slow writer, verify buffered reading assembles complete frame
4. **Connect timeout**: unreachable socket, verify Budget::deadline triggers Cancelled
5. **Read timeout**: connected but silent peer, verify read deadline triggers Cancelled
6. **Write timeout**: connected but full buffer, verify write deadline triggers Cancelled
7. **Pane output subscription**: mock server sends deltas, verify channel receives all
8. **Subscription cancellation**: cancel subscription mid-stream, verify no orphan tasks, no leaked obligations
9. **Two-phase channel safety**: cancel between reserve and send, verify no message loss
10. **Concurrent operations**: multiple requests over same connection, verify serialization
11. **Error recovery**: simulate broken pipe, verify appropriate error propagation

Each test logs: connection events, PDU encode/decode, timeout triggers, channel reserve/commit, scope enter/exit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All DirectMuxClient ops work through asupersync
- PDU wire format bit-for-bit identical (regression test)
- Timeout behavior preserved (5s each)
- Pane output subscription uses structured concurrency
- 11+ tests covering all scenarios
- Obligation leak oracle passes for all tests

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Concurrent client operations testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test concurrent DirectMuxClient operations:
  - Multiple pane subscriptions active simultaneously — verify no cross-talk between subscription channels
  - Interleaved PDU reads and writes on the same connection — verify framing integrity
  - Concurrent connect attempts — verify connection serialization
  - Race between cancellation and PDU commit — verify no partial PDU writes

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for client operation latency**: Add `benches/mux_client_ops.rs` measuring:
  - PDU encode + write_all latency for typical payload sizes (64B, 1KB, 64KB)
  - PDU read_exact + decode latency for typical payload sizes
  - Full round-trip latency (send command PDU → receive response PDU) over Unix socket
  - Pane subscription setup latency (scope.spawn + channel creation)
  - Comparison with tokio-based DirectMuxClient for equivalent operations

## Cross-references
- See wa-brc7d.5 (mux crate in asupersync FrankenTerm crates epic) — DirectMuxClient is the core of the mux crate and its performance characteristics directly determine the crate's viability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:50:15.845522Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:49:29.703468Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-q8vj3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:58.776523Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-2abzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:58.893985Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-1yz79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:59.011099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-1bznu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:59.3449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-p85q","title":"[EPIC] FTUI-01 Architecture Contract and Migration Invariants","description":"## Purpose
Define the non-negotiable migration contract before touching implementation.

## Why
Without a locked architecture and invariants, the migration risks becoming a piecemeal rewrite with hidden regressions.

## Focus
- adopt ftui principles (one-writer, deterministic rendering, inline-first)
- map those principles onto wa’s existing module boundaries and workflows
- codify tradeoffs, risks, and rollback approach

## Output
A decision-complete blueprint future contributors can execute without re-opening old markdown planning artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:05.795864727Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:53:53.209627616Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:53:53.209546886Z","close_reason":"done"}
